clinical_history: 68 year old man with irregular pulse rate. The ECG shows ischemic-appearing T wave inversions with QT prolongation in a patient with severe multivessel coronary artery disease and decreased left ventricular systolic function. Which ONE of the following rhythms is present?
diagnosis: Superficially, this rhythm looks like atrial fibrillation. Closer inspection shows that there are discrete P waves with variable appearance and variable PR intervals. The rate is not fast enough for multifocal atrial tachycardia but shows more variability than the classic wandering atrial pacemaker. The term multifocal atrial rhythm can be used in such cases. This rhythm may be a precursor of atrial fibrillation. The patient was on metoprolol and amiodarone at the time of this ECG and had a history of atrial fibrillation.
